32d398c24a14d17c6051f64cac78a21f11488a72.title: Smooth-Covariate Rate Collapse
32d398c24a14d17c6051f64cac78a21f11488a72.body:
Let $\rho_n$ be the published HOIF benchmark of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}, evaluated at $(n,\alpha,s,d)$. Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive treatment smoothness.)} $\alpha>0$.
\item \textbf{(Positive covariate smoothness.)} $s>0$.
\item \textbf{(Smooth-covariate regime.)} $d\le 4s$.
\item \textbf{(Sample size.)} $n\ge 1$.
\end{itemize}
Then
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)} .
\]

ef7d8d1692c2040b52a932963f93fb59ff4abc46.title: Deficient Regime Algebra

013f478c9d59fa7798eaa15de7daa79141955006.title: Sharp Pointwise Lower Bound
013f478c9d59fa7798eaa15de7daa79141955006.body:
For every covariate dimension $d$ and every real constants
$\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0$, suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive smoothness.)} $\alpha>0$, $\beta>0$, and $s>0$.
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} $M>0$, $c_0>0$, $t_0\in(0,1)$, $\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)$, and the local window determined by $(t_0,\varepsilon_0)$ is contained in $(0,1)$.
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for $(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$.
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant $c>0$ such that, for all sufficiently large sample sizes $n$,
\[
  c\, n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
  \le
  R_n\!\left(
    \mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),
    t_0
  \right),
\]
where $\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$ is the model class of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class} and $R_n$ is the minimax risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.

66bae72fcd34f61672dbf664e86ea70bf47f3d9a.title: Oracle Regime Reduction
66bae72fcd34f61672dbf664e86ea70bf47f3d9a.body:
Let \(d\) be the covariate dimension, and let \(\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\) be constants. Suppose that
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Well-formed constants.)} \(\alpha>0\), \(\beta>0\), \(s>0\), \(M>0\), \(c_0>0\), \(t_0\in(0,1)\), \(\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)\), and the window \([t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) is contained in \((0,1)\).
\item \textbf{(Oracle regime.)} \(d\le 4s\).
\item \textbf{(Baseline slack.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for \(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\).
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large sample sizes \(n\),
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\]
for the published HOIF benchmark rate \(\rho_n\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}, and
\[
c\,\rho_n
\le
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right),
\]
where the model class is Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class} and the minimax risk is Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.

a53a32d2d153eecb95be5bdf8a67d179e12194f1.title: Smooth Covariate Minimax Floor
a53a32d2d153eecb95be5bdf8a67d179e12194f1.body:
Let \(d\in\mathbb N\) and let \(\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R\). Suppose that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Smoothness.)} \(\alpha>0\), \(\beta>0\), and \(s>0\).
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} \(M>0\), \(c_0>0\), \(t_0\in(0,1)\), \(\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)\), and the window \([t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) is contained in \((0,1)\).
\item \textbf{(Smooth-covariate regime.)} \(d\le 4s\).
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for \((d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\).
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\le
R_n\!\left(
\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0
\right),
\]
where \(R_n\) is the minimax risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} over the same-class model of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}. Moreover,
\[
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
=
\rho_n,
\]
with \(\rho_n\) as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}.

e5d68ebc5d7a9615762ab5b81af0f0ed18bacce6.title: All-Beta Oracle Floor
e5d68ebc5d7a9615762ab5b81af0f0ed18bacce6.body:
Fix a covariate dimension $d\in\mathbb N$ and constants
$\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R$. Suppose that:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Positive smoothness.)} $\alpha>0$, $\beta>0$, and $s>0$.
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} $M>0$, $c_0>0$, $t_0\in(0,1)$, $\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)$, and the window of radius $\varepsilon_0$ around $t_0$ lies in the interior dose region.
\item \textbf{(Slack baseline.)} The strict-slack baseline condition of Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for $(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$.
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant $c_{\mathrm{or}}>0$ such that, for all sufficiently large $n\in\mathbb N$,
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\ge
c_{\mathrm{or}}\, n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)} .
\]
Here $\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$ is the model class of Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}, and $R_n$ is the minimax risk of Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk}.

289c087c18efe15f532ea2e9d633f2a0e601c537.title: Deficient Frontier Bracket
289c087c18efe15f532ea2e9d633f2a0e601c537.body:
Fix a covariate dimension $d\in\mathbb N$ and constants
$\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R$.  Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)}  $\alpha>0$, $\beta>0$, $s>0$, $M>0$, $c_0>0$, $t_0\in(0,1)$, $\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)$, and the window $[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]$ lies in $(0,1)$.
\item \textbf{(Deficient covariate smoothness.)}  $4s<d$.
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)}  Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for $(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)$.
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant $c>0$ such that, for all sufficiently large sample sizes $n$,
\[
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\le
R_n\!\left(
\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0
\right),
\]
where the minimax risk is as in Definition~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and the class is Definition~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}.  Moreover, for the published HOIF benchmark of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate},
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-\,2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\]
and its exponent is strictly smaller than the certified lower-bound exponent:
\[
\frac{2}{1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha}
<
\frac{2\alpha}{2\alpha+1}.
\]

41a9811031175f1fad80a4f2033e3cf5ef86a56d.title: Certified Beta Frontier Bracket
41a9811031175f1fad80a4f2033e3cf5ef86a56d.body:
Let \(d\in\mathbb N\), and let \(\alpha,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\in\mathbb R\). Assume:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Regime constants.)} \(\alpha>0\), \(\beta>0\), \(s>0\), \(M>0\), \(c_0>0\), \(t_0\in(0,1)\), \(\varepsilon_0\in(0,1/2)\), and the window \([t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) lies in \((0,1)\).
\item \textbf{(Strict-slack baseline.)} Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:baseline-submodel-slack} holds for \(d,\beta,s,M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0\).
\end{itemize}
Then there exists a constant \(c>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\le
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right),
\]
where the minimax risk and the same-class Hölder dose-response model are those of Definitions~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and~\ref{obj:def:holder-dose-class}. Moreover, for every \(n\ge 1\), the benchmark \(\rho_n\) of Definition~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate} satisfies
\[
d\le 4s
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\rho_n=n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)},
\]
and
\[
4s<d
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\rho_n=n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\quad\text{and}\quad
\frac{2}{1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha}
<
\frac{2\alpha}{2\alpha+1}.
\]

da1fec8a622fd5277231244f27c98b24a4a7adc1.title: Iid sampling
da1fec8a622fd5277231244f27c98b24a4a7adc1.body: The observations \(O_1,\ldots,O_n\) are iid draws from \(P\).

0aad3c0aea692847a06fb950ceaa8466e077399b.title: No unmeasured confounding
0aad3c0aea692847a06fb950ceaa8466e077399b.body: For every \(a\in[0,1]\), the potential outcome \(Y(a)\) is independent of \(A\) conditional on \(X\) under \(P\).

5a43ba8ffd30a4a0eeb176ba4ef96642b1e3767d.title: Interior dose
5a43ba8ffd30a4a0eeb176ba4ef96642b1e3767d.body:
The exposure neighborhood around \(t_0\) is contained in the interior of the exposure support:
\[
[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\subset(0,1).
\]

17d66cb2eb6fabce6c42752b4741e942810c5f38.title: Local positivity
17d66cb2eb6fabce6c42752b4741e942810c5f38.body:
For every \(a\in[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0]\) and every \(x\in[0,1]^d\),
\[
\pi_P(a\mid x)\ge c_0.
\]

5efb9fce77e8b9e092c383aabee1e557abbac973.title: Treatment-regression dose smoothness
5efb9fce77e8b9e092c383aabee1e557abbac973.body:
For every \(x\in[0,1]^d\), the map
\[
a\longmapsto \mu_P(a,x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^\alpha(M;[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0])\).

cb18411e4fd72b2f89b7238fec903622fb9280b1.title: Treatment-density dose smoothness
cb18411e4fd72b2f89b7238fec903622fb9280b1.body:
For every \(x\in[0,1]^d\), the map
\[
a\longmapsto \pi_P(a\mid x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^\beta(M;[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0])\).

c298884750571adc1b3fddd86978543ae381ffe2.title: Treatment-regression covariate smoothness
c298884750571adc1b3fddd86978543ae381ffe2.body:
The map
\[
x\longmapsto \mu_P(t_0,x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^s(M;[0,1]^d)\).

14d5e3e149c723f2e62a6b11235240f429ef18ff.title: Treatment-density covariate smoothness
14d5e3e149c723f2e62a6b11235240f429ef18ff.body:
The map
\[
x\longmapsto \pi_P(t_0\mid x)
\]
belongs to the Hölder ball \(\mathcal H^s(M;[0,1]^d)\).

d27523d85645d99ede0a03fccad0b76c5d4fe522.title: Covariate-density smoothness
d27523d85645d99ede0a03fccad0b76c5d4fe522.body:
The marginal covariate density \(p_{X,P}\) belongs to the Hölder ball
\[
\mathcal H^s(M;[0,1]^d)
\]
and satisfies
\[
p_{X,P}(x)\le M
\]
on \([0,1]^d\).

00ab6cc8fa3d63ac33a9661e9e022129dc506409.title: Baseline submodel slack
00ab6cc8fa3d63ac33a9661e9e022129dc506409.body:
There exist a density \(p_0\) on \([0,1]^d\), a conditional treatment density \(q_0\) on \([0,1]\), and a constant \(\eta_0>0\) such that:
\[
\|p_0\|_{\mathcal H^s([0,1]^d)}\le M-\eta_0,
\]
\(q_0\) has Hölder norm at most \(M-\eta_0\) in the smoothness class required of the treatment density \(\pi_P\),
\[
q_0(a)\ge c_0+\eta_0
\qquad\text{for all }a\in[t_0-\varepsilon_0,t_0+\varepsilon_0],
\]
and
\[
p_0(x)\le M-\eta_0
\]
on \([0,1]^d\).

ef4fa2efc36855b8e9915e5b8075504f7c671bfe.title: Dose-response partial mean $\theta_P(t_0)$
ef4fa2efc36855b8e9915e5b8075504f7c671bfe.body:
The dose-response partial mean at the evaluation dose $t_0$ is
\[
\theta_P(t_0)
=
\int_{[0,1]^d} \mu_P(t_0,x)\,p_{X,P}(x)\,dx .
\]

575f718ae491ee294aee45cc9301f0a4b6f1b75e.title: Minimax risk $R_n(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0)$
575f718ae491ee294aee45cc9301f0a4b6f1b75e.body:
The minimax risk over the model class
\(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\) at the evaluation dose \(t_0\) is
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
=
\inf_{\hat\theta_n}
\sup_{P\in \mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)}
E_P\!\left[
\left\{\hat\theta_n-\theta_P(t_0)\right\}^2
\right],
\]
where the infimum is over measurable estimators \(\hat\theta_n\) based on
\(O_1,\ldots,O_n\).

ea7ec8d45c6f66244e4f8b3baf523c218a9dec48.title: Published HOIF benchmark $\rho_n$
ea7ec8d45c6f66244e4f8b3baf523c218a9dec48.body:
The published higher-order influence-function benchmark rate is
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\vee
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)} .
\]

cc3ac1bbcb556228140a08552a613a7d3f0023e6.title: Beta frontier handle $\mathfrak B_{\alpha,\beta,s}$
cc3ac1bbcb556228140a08552a613a7d3f0023e6.body:
The beta comparison handle \(\mathfrak B_{\alpha,\beta,s}\) denotes the following frontier statement. For every \(\beta>0\), a two-point Le Cam construction perturbing only the treatment regression \(\mu_P\), with the covariate density and treatment-density baselines fixed, yields the all-\(\beta\) lower floor
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\ge
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)} .
\]
When \(s\ge d/4\), the published benchmark \(\rho_n\) has this same exponent, so the converse is on the classical interior treatment-regression scale. When \(0<s<d/4\),
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\]
is recorded only as a published upper-side comparator, not as a same-class certified upper endpoint. The remaining unrestricted upper-frontier question is whether \(\rho_n\) is attainable on the original class, or whether an intermediate or genuinely \(\beta\)-sensitive phase intervenes.

2a056fc679f273686a0a03d744eed51158f59b41.title: Full beta frontier
2a056fc679f273686a0a03d744eed51158f59b41.body:
Under the baseline-submodel slack condition, the original class
\(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\) satisfies the all-\(\beta\) lower floor
\[
R_n\!\left(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0\right)
\ge
c\,n^{-2\alpha/(2\alpha+1)}
\]
for all sufficiently large \(n\). When \(s\ge d/4\), this lower floor has the same exponent as \(\rho_n\). When \(0<s<d/4\),
\[
\rho_n
=
n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/\alpha)}
\]
has a strictly smaller exponent and remains only a published upper-side comparator. The open comparison question \(\mathfrak Q_{\alpha,\beta,s}\) is whether the original class admits an upper bound of order \(\rho_n\), an intermediate exponent, or a genuinely \(\beta\)-sensitive phase.

422f136950a897073bf7a4eac72fc7024ccc27ec.title: Bernoulli mean-channel KL bound
422f136950a897073bf7a4eac72fc7024ccc27ec.body:
Fix \(B>0\). Let \(Q_u\) and \(Q_v\) be distributions on \(\{-B,B\}\) with means \(u\) and \(v\), respectively, so that
\[
Q_u(B)=\frac{1+u/B}{2},
\qquad
Q_v(B)=\frac{1+v/B}{2}.
\]
If \(|u|\le B/2\) and \(|v|\le B/2\), then
\[
\mathrm{KL}(Q_u,Q_v)
\le
\frac{2(u-v)^2}{B^2}.
\]

c139fcff65e747601c8cd0e19c2733627076d6a4.title: Le Cam Two-Point Bound
c139fcff65e747601c8cd0e19c2733627076d6a4.body:
Let \(Q_0\) and \(Q_1\) be two laws for the \(n\)-sample experiment, and let \(\theta_0\) and \(\theta_1\) be the corresponding values of a real parameter. Set
\[
\Delta = |\theta_1-\theta_0|.
\]
If
\[
\mathrm{KL}(Q_0,Q_1)\le K<\infty,
\]
then there exists a constant \(c_K>0\), depending only on \(K\), such that every estimator \(T\) satisfies
\[
\max_{i=0,1} E_{Q_i}\!\left[(T-\theta_i)^2\right]\ge c_K \Delta^2 .
\]

55f47fd9767793228c996d9816cbc9e42ace0899.title: Published HOIF Comparator
55f47fd9767793228c996d9816cbc9e42ace0899.body:
It is known from Bonvini--Kennedy (2022) that the published higher-order influence-function estimator attains the benchmark rate \(\rho_n\) on the Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class: there exists a constant \(C>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\),
\[
R_n \le C \rho_n .
\]
This assertion is for the external Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class, under additional localized regularity conditions, and not for the same-class Hölder dose model \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\).

a8871a8f3c411de6b387b3344164a514fd82b816.title: Hölder Dose Class \(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)\)
a8871a8f3c411de6b387b3344164a514fd82b816.body:
With the minimax risk \(R_n(\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0),t_0)\) and the published HOIF benchmark \(\rho_n\) as in Definitions~\ref{obj:def:minimax-risk} and~\ref{obj:def:published-hoif-rate}, define
\[
\mathcal P_{\alpha,\beta,s}(M,c_0,\varepsilon_0,t_0)
\]
to be the collection of laws \(P\) satisfying all of the following conditions:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{(Sampling.)} The observations satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:iid-sampling}.
\item \textbf{(Causal identification.)} Consistency and no unmeasured confounding hold as in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:consistency} and~\ref{obj:ass:no-unmeasured-confounding}.
\item \textbf{(Outcome boundedness.)} The bounded-outcome condition holds as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:bounded-outcome}.
\item \textbf{(Interior dose.)} The evaluation dose \(t_0\) and local radius \(\varepsilon_0\) satisfy Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:interior-dose}.
\item \textbf{(Local positivity.)} The treatment density obeys the local positivity condition with constant \(c_0\) as in Assumption~\ref{obj:ass:local-positivity}.
\item \textbf{(Treatment-direction smoothness.)} The maps \(a\mapsto \mu_P(a,x)\) and \(a\mapsto \pi_P(a\mid x)\) satisfy the treatment-direction Hölder conditions in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:mu-treatment-holder} and~\ref{obj:ass:pi-treatment-holder}.
\item \textbf{(Covariate-direction smoothness.)} The relevant covariate functions for \(\mu_P\), \(\pi_P\), and \(p_{X,P}\) satisfy the Hölder conditions in Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:mu-covariate-holder}, \ref{obj:ass:pi-covariate-holder}, and~\ref{obj:ass:px-holder}.
\end{itemize}

3f7f04a2203e3d18ff1e68313ba19dde029f6667.detail: \mathcal H^\gamma(M;D) [no-anchor] in ass:mu-treatment-holder/ass:pi-treatment-holder/ass:mu-covariate-holder/ass:pi-covariate-holder/ass:px-holder/ass:baseline-submodel-slack — add a definitionv for the Hölder ball and Hölder norm notation, then reference it from every assumption using \mathcal H or \|\cdot\|_{\mathcal H}

\mu_P(a,x) [no-anchor] in ass:mu-treatment-holder/ass:mu-covariate-holder/def:theta-functional/def:holder-dose-class — add a definitionv defining \mu_P(a,x) as the treatment regression and reference it from its assumptions and the theta/model-class definitions

\pi_P(a\mid x) [no-anchor] in ass:local-positivity/ass:pi-treatment-holder/ass:pi-covariate-holder/ass:baseline-submodel-slack/def:holder-dose-class — add a definitionv defining \pi_P(a\mid x) as the conditional treatment density and reference it from positivity, smoothness, slack, and model-class statements

p_{X,P}(x) [undefined] in ass:px-holder/def:theta-functional/def:holder-dose-class — add a definitionv for the marginal covariate density p_{X,P} and reference it from the covariate-density assumption and theta/model-class definitions

\mathrm{KL} [undefined] in lem:bernoulli-mean-channel-kl/lem:le-cam-two-point-mse-source — add a definitionv for Kullback--Leibler divergence \mathrm{KL}(Q_0,Q_1), or explicitly cite an anchored definition, and reference it from both KL lemmas

Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class [undefined] in lem:published-upper-bound-cited — add an anchored citedv or definitionv specifying the external Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class, or rephrase the comparator so it does not present an unresolved law family

1a5b81dd019a076b31c1bd17966346d9576d85a0.detail: \mathcal H^\gamma(M;\mathcal Z) [no-anchor] in ass:mu-treatment-holder/ass:pi-treatment-holder/ass:mu-covariate-holder/ass:pi-covariate-holder/ass:px-holder/ass:baseline-submodel-slack — add a definitionv for the Hölder ball \mathcal H^\gamma(M;\mathcal Z), including its domain and radius conventions, and \ref it from each assumption that uses it

\|\cdot\|_{\mathcal H^s([0,1]^d)} [undefined] in ass:baseline-submodel-slack — define the Hölder norm notation in the same Hölder-class definitionv, or add a separate anchored definitionv for the norm and reference it from ass:baseline-submodel-slack
\mathrm{KL} [undefined] in lem:bernoulli-mean-channel-kl/lem:le-cam-two-point-mse-source — add a definitionv for Kullback-Leibler divergence \mathrm{KL}(Q,R) and reference it from both KL lemmas

R_n [mismatch] in lem:published-upper-bound-cited — replace the bare R_n in the cited upper-bound statement by a fully parameterized risk, or define and reference a separate risk notation for the Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class

Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class [undefined] in lem:published-upper-bound-cited — add an anchored cited definition identifying the exact Bonvini--Kennedy localized-regularity class and reference it from lem:published-upper-bound-cited
